name: Lucky Charms Protein Cookies
description: Lucky Charms flavor of the Crumbl base — base chassis + almond extract. The Lucky Charms cereal marshmallows are the topping and are intentionally omitted (matches the source 'without the lucky charms'). NOTE: with the marshmallows omitted, this is effectively identical to Birthday Cake — the marshmallow topping is the only real differentiator, a one-line add when wanted. Derived off Crumbl Base Protein Cookie. Untested at this base chassis.

payload.text: 9 min is the pull point, not a guideline — protein-yogurt cookies keep cooking on the hot pan. Centers will look underdone; that's correct. They firm to a soft, cakey texture as they cool. Going past 10 min makes them dry and tight.
The 20–30 min chill is real work here — this dough has only 1 tbsp butter and barely spreads. Chilling firms it so the flattened mounds hold their shape; skipping it yields flat, patchy cookies.

Add the almond milk 1 tsp at a time — the dough is correct when it just holds together and pulls away from the bowl. Stop early; too much liquid makes flat cookies that don't hold shape after flattening.
The cookies must be fully cool before frosting — the Cool-Whip frosting melts on a warm cookie and soaks in rather than sitting on top.
Fold the Cool Whip in first, then beat in the dry ingredients — adding protein powder directly to un-folded Cool Whip deflates it and the frosting turns dense and pasty instead of light.
